name: LineChart

unions.name: ChartModel
unions.comment:
Need this for determining if a point on the chart has been hovered on. This should be a part of your model.

    type Model =
      { otherData : OtherData
      , chartModel : ChartModel
      }

unions.name: ChartMsg
unions.comment:
Used to handle tooltips for the line chart. This should be added to your own msg type.

    type Msg
      = Msg1 Data1
      | Msg2 Data2
      | ChartMessage ChartMsg

unions.name: LineChartData
unions.comment: Represents the data that will be used to draw the Line Chart. Many lines can be drawn in one chart.

unions.name: LineChartDataSet
unions.comment: Represents one line of the Line Chart.

unions.name: LineChartOptions
unions.comment: Used to configure the line chart.

values.name: createData
createDataSet
values.comment:
Create a LineChartDataSet by providing:
1. A label for the dataset
2. A list of values
3. A color which will be used to plot this dataset on the chart. [Click here for the color module](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/tesk9/palette/latest/TransparentColor).

One dataset corresponds to one line on the chart.

    import Palette.X11 exposing (black)
    import TransparentColor exposing (opaque, fromColor)

    serverCost :: LineChartDataSet
    serverCost = createDataSet "Server Cost" [ 20.4, 30.2, 10] (fromColor opaque black)

values.name: defaultLineChartOptions
values.comment:
Default options for configuring the line chart.

    options : LineChartOptions
    options =
      setChartWidth 1200 defaultLineChartOptions
      |> setChartHeight 800
      |> setTooltipWidth 200
      |> setTooltipHeight 100
      |> setLabelWidth 200
      |> setLabelHeight 200
      |> setPointRadius 4

values.name: initChartModel
lineChart
values.comment:
Used to draw the line chart. The arguments are:
1. Options to configure the line chart.
2. The data to be displayed on the chart.
3. A msg constructor to convert ChartMsg into your own msg type.
4. The chart model, which should be stored inside your Elm model.

Following code shows how to setup parts 3 and 4.

    import LineChart exposing (lineChart)

    type Model =
      { otherData : OtherData
      , chartModel : ChartModel
      }

    type Msg
      = Msg1 Data1
      | Msg2 Data2
      | ChartMessage ChartMsg

    init : Model
    init =
      { otherData = {...}
      , chartModel = initChartModel
      }

    view : Model -> Html Msg
    view model = lineChart defaultLineChartOptions data ChartMessage model.chartModel

    update : Msg -> Model -> Model
    update msg model =
      case msg of
        ChartMessage chartMsg ->
          { model
          | chartModel = updateChartModel chartMsg model.chartModel
          }
        NoOp -> model
values.type: LineChart.LineChartOptions -> LineChart.LineChartData -> (LineChart.ChartMsg -> msg) -> LineChart.ChartModel -> Html.Html msg

values.name: setLabelRotation
values.comment:
Set the Label Rotation in degrees.
1. If you provide rotation value (even if it is `Just 0`), label will be rotated from its end, counter-clockwise.
2. If you don't provide a value (Nothing), label will be center aligned with its axis.

values.name: setLegendSpacing
values.comment: Set the spacing between two legend keys.

values.name: setLegendWidth
values.comment: Set the width of one legend key.

values.name: setPointRadius
values.comment: Set the point radius on the chart. Hovered points double their radius.

values.name: updateChartModel
values.comment:
Used to update the ChartModel type. This should be used inside your update function.

    update : Msg -> Model -> Model
    update msg model =
      case msg of
        ChartMessage chartMsg ->
          { model
          | chartModel = updateChartModel chartMsg model.chartModel
          }
        NoOp -> model
